features.summary: American Tower reported strong Q4 2021 results with consolidated AFFO per share growth of 13.7% and property revenue growth of 13%. The company completed major acquisitions including Telxius in Europe and CoreSite data centers, deploying over $20 billion in M&A. They constructed a record 6,400 new communication sites globally and maintained their long-term targets of averaging at least 4% U.S. organic tenant billings growth and 10% AFFO per share growth through 2027.
features.management_outlook: Management expects 2022 property revenue of over $10.3 billion (13% growth) and adjusted EBITDA of over $6.5 billion (10% growth). They anticipate strong new leasing activity with gross new business contributions nearly 7% higher than 2021, driven by comprehensive MLAs and 5G deployments. The company plans to construct 6,500 new sites in 2022 and expects acceleration in U.S. organic growth between 2023-2027 as Sprint churn impacts fade and 5G investments intensify.
features.risks: The company faces elevated churn in 2022, including $160 million in Sprint churn impacts and carrier consolidation events in international markets. Foreign exchange headwinds are expected to negatively impact results by approximately $125 million in property revenue. The CoreSite acquisition financing strategy remains uncertain, with assumptions of $5 billion in common equity issuance that could be dilutive to shareholders depending on market conditions and final structure.
features.key_drivers: 5G network deployments are driving significant growth opportunities, with U.S. carriers spending $105 billion on mid-band spectrum and over $65 billion in global network CapEx. Mobile data consumption is expected to grow over 25% annually in the U.S. and higher internationally over the next five years. The CoreSite acquisition positions the company for edge computing opportunities, while record new site construction continues generating attractive returns with average day 1 NOI yields of nearly 12%.
